source.title: Abnormalities in the awareness and control of action.
source.venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B

confidence_rationale: Canonical statement of the comparator model of agency attribution. The architecture it specifies -- a forward model that predicts sensory consequences of a motor command, a comparator that flags the mismatch between prediction and reafference, and the proposal that delusions of control arise when this comparator signal is corrupted -- is a direct biological precedent for a single-pass E2_harm_s forward model that generates a predicted next-state and compares it to observed next-state. This is cleaner than the retired SD-003 two-pass counterfactual (actual vs counterfactual action rollouts) and matches the biology more closely. Confidence moderate-high because the model is theoretical and draws on multiple experimental strands, and because the signal is described in generic sensorimotor terms rather than as a harm-specific mechanism.
failure_signatures: The comparator signal as described in Frith 2000 is generic sensorimotor (proprioceptive and exteroceptive feedback) -- it does not specifically predict harm or nociceptive outcomes. Mapping to E2_harm_s requires an inference step that the forward-model-plus-comparator architecture extends to a harm-specific channel, which the paper does not establish.

The paper emphasises post-hoc comparison of actual and predicted sensory consequences. If the REE agent needs the comparator signal to gate attribution *before* the outcome settles (e.g. as part of commitment-boundary logic), the biological precedent is weaker.

Schizophrenia delusions of control are attributed to impairment in representing intended movements -- a failure mode in the predictor. The paper does not strongly dissociate predictor failure from comparator failure. REE would need separate diagnostics for each.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Awareness and control of action rest on a forward model that predicts the sensory consequences of a motor command. A comparator flags the discrepancy between predicted and actual sensory feedback. Delusions of control in schizophrenia reflect failures in this comparator or its inputs: patients can no longer properly link intentions to actions.
mapping.ree_translation: The REE successor to the retired two-pass SD-003 pipeline should be a single-pass comparator: E2_harm_s(z_harm_s_t, a_t) produces z_harm_s_pred, and the residual z_harm_s_obs - z_harm_s_pred is the agency signal. Action-caused changes are what the agent predicted; world-caused changes are what it did not. This inherits Frith 2000's architecture directly and drops the counterfactual rollout a_cf that z_world-only E2 made infeasible under SD-010 / SD-011.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Frith 2000 frames the comparator on generic sensorimotor reafference -- tactile, proprioceptive, visual consequences of limb movement. E2_harm_s operates on a narrower, more affectively-loaded channel (nociceptive proximity / harm_obs_s). The transfer assumes the comparator architecture is substrate-independent, i.e. that any predictable reafferent channel can host it. This is plausible (the cerebellar forward model is not modality-specific) but not directly shown by Frith 2000.
mapping.source_context: Review in Philosophical Transactions B summarising the Frith-Blakemore-Wolpert programme on motor awareness and its breakdown in schizophrenia. Integrates experimental findings from the same lab (e.g. the tickle studies, force-matching) into a unified forward-model / comparator account.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (PhilTransB, highly cited canonical review by the architects of the comparator model). Mapping fidelity moderate-high because the architecture transfers cleanly but the channel (generic sensorimotor vs harm-specific) differs. Transfer risk moderate-low: the forward-model / comparator motif is domain-general in computational terms, and REE already assumes a learnable E2_harm_s in ARC-033.
